top_categories.workout_context: Race-pace efforts that mimic Gran Fondo Siete Lagos' demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before March.
Gran Fondo Siete Lagos' mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 62 miles.
When someone attacks on mixed terrain, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
79ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
At Gran Fondo Siete Lagos, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Gran Fondo Siete Lagos. Train it until it feels boring.
62 miles on mixed terrain burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Gran Fondo Siete Lagos, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mixed terrain. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Varied gradients on mixed terrain demand climbing versatility. Sit the shallow stuff, stand the steep stuff, switch without thinking.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 4,920ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.
Double-threshold training builds sustained power with less fatigue. More FTP per hour of training.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for Gran Fondo Siete Lagos’s 62 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Gran Fondo Siete Lagos. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Gran Fondo Siete Lagos requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on mixed terrain to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Race Simulation, Gravel Specific, and VO2max to prepare you for 62 miles of mixed terrain in San Martín de los Andes, Neuquén, Argentina.
